name: RV pacing-associated AF risk increase
description: For each 1% increase in cumulative right ventricular (RV) pacing, there is a corresponding 1% increase in risk of atrial fibrillation (AF). This metric quantifies the direct relationship between RV pacing frequency and AF risk, providing a measurable threshold for clinical decision-making. The metric is bounded by the cumulative percentage of RV pacing and is used to assess pacing strategies in cardiac device management.
